_comment:
Aspect catalog. One registry, three sections — same code shape for all entries (the `category` recorded on each Aspect record reflects the section it was parsed under). Sub-aspect nesting is preserved within a section: a card carrying `pine` satisfies `aspect.wood.min: 1` because pine is nested under wood. Sub-aspects inherit `icon` and `color` from the nearest declaring ancestor — omit a field to inherit. `icon` and `color` are OPTIONAL throughout (default `""` and `0x888888`) — entries with no display intent (most traits) just leave them off. Labels and descriptions are NOT stored here — they live in `content/locales/aspects/<lang>.json`, keyed flat by aspect name, and inherit along the parent chain at lookup time (`aspectLabel` / `aspectDescription`). The three sections:

  • `aspects` — WHAT a card has. Resources, materials, soul stats. Primary recipe inputs. Displayed in details (always, including the minimum/collapsed state).
  • `features` — HOW a card acts. Behavioural / functional tags (faction, fleeting, level, crafting, speed, inventory). Recipe-matchable too, just less commonly. Displayed in details only when expanded; rendered above the description in a smaller pip row.
  • `traits` — WHAT a card is. Descriptive properties read by simulation code (movement cost, height, climate envelopes, placement metadata). Not displayed. Recipe-matchable via the same `aspect.<name>.min: N` predicate (values are f32 so float predicates work).

Values on cards are f32 (the same value can be `2`, `2.0`, or `1.2` — author's choice; integers parse without loss).
